# dogshow.lol — Codebase Reference

## ⚠️ CRITICAL: DO NOT EDIT DogShowPrototype.jsx

DogShowPrototype.jsx is an OLD React prototype from early brainstorming. It is **NOT** the live site. The live site is **static HTML/CSS/JS** spread across multiple files listed below. If you need to make changes to the site, edit the HTML/CSS/JS files directly. Never treat DogShowPrototype.jsx as the codebase.

---

## Tech Stack

- **Frontend**: Multi-page static HTML/CSS/JS (no framework, no build step for the static pages; `api/og.tsx` is a TS/JSX Edge function compiled by Vercel via `tsconfig.json`)
- **Real-time**: PartyKit WebSocket — host: `dogshow.schemestudio.partykit.dev`, room: `dogshow-live`
- **Payments**: Stripe Checkout via PartyKit endpoint `/create-checkout`. **Free-to-enter (2026-06-23): entering a dog is free; money only buys bones.** Two live SKUs (price IDs hard-coded in `party/server.js` `handleCreateCheckout`): `general` $1.99 (+250 bones — "Dog Entry Pro" + the repeatable bones pack), `premium_plus` $5.99 ("Top Dog" — +1000 bones + slot booking + 3× stage). The `premium` $3.99 "Enter Your Dog" SKU is RETIRED (archived in Stripe).
- **Hosting**: Vercel (migrated from GitHub Pages, 2026-05-22 — commits `a8ce1b0` + `c6185a6`). Repo `SCHEMESTUDIO/dogshow`, custom domain via CNAME. PartyKit hosts the real-time/API server; Vercel hosts the static files + the `api/dog.js` SSR function for `/d/{slug}` (audit #27) + the `api/og.tsx` Edge function for per-dog OG images. `vercel.json` defines `outputDirectory: "."` and rewrites: `/d/:slug` → `/api/dog?slug=:slug`, and `/:slug` → `/:slug.html` (clean URLs no longer require filename=URL since GH Pages no longer serves). `.vercelignore` excludes `party/`, `*.md`, `*.docx`, `*.py`, `DogShowPrototype.jsx`, `preview-redesign.html`, and `scripts/` so they don't ship to the public site. `package.json` declares a no-op `build` script so Vercel actually runs `npm install` (without one, install was sometimes skipped, leaving Edge functions unable to resolve `@vercel/og`).
- **Error tracking**: Sentry on both browser (loader injected by `analytics.js` on every page) and PartyKit server (DSN in `party/server.js`, `reportToSentry()` wraps the request dispatcher). Audit High-3. **Verify DNS / which host is actually live before asserting** — vercel.json is committed but the cutover to Vercel's edge network was not directly verified by this audit.
- **Deploy-drift check (added 2026-06-04, post-UX-audit)**: PartyKit prod can silently drift from the repo (root cause of UX-audit H1/H2 — see `UX-AUDIT-2026-06-04.md`). To catch it, `party/build-info.js` exports a `BUILD_INFO` placeholder that `party/stamp-version.cjs` overwrites with the real git commit + dirty flag + timestamp at deploy time (then reverts locally so git stays clean). The PartyKit `deploy` script is now `node stamp-version.cjs && npx partykit deploy && git checkout -- build-info.js`. The baked version is served at **`GET /version`**; `npm run check-deploy` (`party/check-deploy.cjs`) compares live prod `/version` against local `git HEAD` and prints in-sync / drifted. **Run `npm run check-deploy` from `party/` after any PartyKit deploy** to confirm prod matches the repo. **Current prod state (verified 2026-07-13):** `GET /version` returns `{"commit":"32ea0ca","dirty":true,"deployedAt":"2026-07-08T15:22:27Z"}` — a **dirty** PartyKit deploy shipped 2026-07-08 with uncommitted working-tree changes. Main HEAD is `2d6451b` (email-cadence consolidation, committed 2026-07-08 16:28 — *after* that 15:22 deploy). ⚠️ **Unverified whether the cadence changes were in that dirty deploy's working tree or exist only in the later commit** — before trusting the once-a-week email policy is live, redeploy PartyKit from a clean `2d6451b`+ and re-check `/version`. `2d6451b` is also **unpushed** (local main ahead 1 of origin as of 2026-07-13).
- **Chat LLM**: Claude Haiku 4.5 (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`) powers the responsive `sir_barks_alot` chat bot. Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in PartyKit env; feature is OFF if unset (bot falls back to canned messages like the others). See "Responsive Chat Bot" section below.
- **Analytics**: Google Analytics `G-V830P7PPHQ` + Faurya Analytics (website ID `cmosekmvz000xl204a7bhm4cm`). **Consent-gated** as of 2026-06-04 — GA, Faurya, and Bing UET no longer load inline; they're injected by `analytics.js` only after consent. See "Cookie Consent (GDPR)" below.
- **Font**: Custom "Yang Bagus" (`YangBagus.ttf`)
- **Colors (SITEWIDE RECOLOR 2026-07-08 — `81d3623` + `8504a77` + `46e8516` + `32ea0ca`)**: the site flipped from the old dark-purple theme to a **pale-lavender / light theme** — pale lavender bg (`--bg: #f3eefb`), white cards (`--bg-card: #ffffff`), warm variant `--bg-warm: #ece3f8`, dark-purple text (`--text-bright: #2a2150`), link-purple accent (`--accent: #5b46d6`, `--accent-dark: #6a5acd`), WCAG-darkened. **The old orange `#FF8C42` and dark bg `#0f0a22`/`#1a1035` are gone** (`--live-dot` keeps `#7B68EE`). Follow-ups in the recolor set: hero wordmark → dark purple (`8504a77`); breed titles → dark purple + square/no-crop dog framing on stage/breed-hero/gallery/upload (`46e8516`); no orange text on lilac — headings/labels/stats → dark purple, bare links → link purple incl. Hall of Bones (`32ea0ca`). Note `.landing-body` still declares a stray `color:#fff` in style.css.
- **CSS vars**: Defined in `:root` in style.css (`--bg`, `--bg-card`, `--bg-warm`, `--accent`, `--accent-dark`, `--text-bright`, `--text-dim`, `--text-faint`, `--border`, `--curtain*`, etc.)- **Auth**: Magic-link via Resend (`POST /login` emails a one-time `loginUrl` carrying a 60-min `magic:` token; `login.html?token=...` exchanges it for a 30-day session token). localStorage stores `dogshow_token`, `dogshow_tier`, `dogshow_username`, `dogshow_fan_id`. **In-show signup** (added 2026-05-26): `show.html` has an inline `#registerOverlay` that takes name+email and registers instantly (no magic link round-trip) — used when an unregistered visitor tries to chat or send a bone.
- **Email compliance (CAN-SPAM)**: Every user-facing email goes through `unsubscribeFooter()` in `party/server.js`, which appends a one-click unsubscribe link plus a physical mailing address (`222 Spaniel Dr, Morrisville, NC 27560, USA`, set in `ADDR_LINE`). `user.unsubscribed=true` is checked before sending marketing/recap mail. Footer added `543d478`; unsubscribe flow added `8651797`. **Link format changed 2026-06-04 (UX-audit H1)**: the link is now a **single opaque param `?x=<userId>.<token>`** (token = first 16 hex of `SHA-256(userId + ':' + RESEND_API_KEY + ':unsub_v1')`). Root cause of H1: the mail-delivery pipeline (Resend) corrupted the **second** query param's `=` in the old two-param `?u=<userId>&t=<token>` link, breaking unsubscribe in *every* email; collapsing to one param sidesteps it. The `/unsubscribe` handler still accepts the legacy `?u=..&t=..` form for links already sitting in inboxes.
- **Sitewide footer (standardized 2026-06-04, `31c84c6` + `2620b7b`)**: every page (and the SSR `api/dog.js` / `api/breed.js` templates) now carries the same standardized footer — nav links (Home, Watch, About, Contact, All Dogs), legal links (Privacy, Terms, `[data-cookie-settings]` "Cookie settings"), two hub links — **Guides** (`/resources`) and **Breeds** (`/breeds`) — which replaced the five individual SEO-landing-page links on 2026-06-18 (footer was getting crowded; the individual contest/guide pages now live behind the `/resources` hub), plus Scheme Studio + PartyKit credit links. **Edit footers as a sitewide set, not page-by-page** — a footer link added to one page should be added to all to keep them consistent.

## Real Testimonials — Reply-by-Email + Admin Curation (added 2026-06-01)

The landing-page testimonial rail used to carry 3 fabricated quotes. Google policy forbids fake endorsements, so the rail is now driven by real, admin-approved testimonials sent in by owners after their dog airs.

**Flow:**
1. **Trigger** — when a community dog's `firstAppearedAt` flips from `null` to a timestamp (one of two spots in `advanceDog()` in `party/server.js`: the slot-fire path and the every-5th-dog rotation path), `maybeSendTestimonialRequest(dog)` fires as fire-and-forget. Guard key `testimonialRequest:<dogId>` is set in storage *before* the send so a transient failure can't cause a re-send the next time the dog airs. Already-aired dogs at deploy time are NOT backfilled — James asks the first 3-5 paid fans directly.
2. **Request email** — `sendTestimonialRequestEmail(email, dog)`, Wodehouse-genial tone, single ask. Subject embeds `[ref:TOK-xxxxxxxx]` where the token is `SHA-256(dogId + ':' + RESEND_API_KEY + ':testimonial_v1')` truncated to 12 hex (deterministic, unforgeable, no extra storage). `Reply-To` set to `INBOUND_REPLY_TO` env var (default `replies@dogshow.lol`). Respects `_isUnsubscribed`.
3. **Inbound webhook** — `POST /inbound-email?secret=<INBOUND_WEBHOOK_SECRET>`. Accepts a generic `{from, subject, text, html?}` JSON shape (works with Resend Inbound, Postmark, CloudMailin — anything that posts an inbound-email webhook). Fail-closed if env var is unset. `_resolveTestimonialDog()` matches the token in subject/body to a dog; `_stripQuotedReply()` removes quoted reply headers + `--` signatures + "Sent from my iPhone"; `_sanitizeTestimonialText()` strips HTML and caps at 280 chars. Stored as `status: 'pending'`. Triggers an admin alert to James.
4. **Admin moderation** — `admin.html` renders pending testimonials with an editable textarea + Approve/Reject buttons. Manual-add form lets James paste a testimonial directly for a specific dog (default status `approved`) — used both as fallback for parsing misses AND to seed the first 3-5 testimonials before inbound is wired.
5. **Public read** — `GET /testimonials` (5-min `Cache-Control`) returns approved entries; `index.html` fetches on load. **Both the top-of-page ticker AND the in-page testimonials section are gated behind ≥3 approved testimonials** (raised from 1 → 3 on 2026-06-02 in `50fbe34` — a single quote felt thinner than no quote). Until then both stay `display: none`. Cap rendered cards at 6 on the in-page rail.

**Storage:** single key `testimonials` = array of `{id, dogId, slug, dogName, username, email, text, receivedAt, status, moderatedAt?, source: 'reply'|'manual', original?, rawSubject?}`.

**Env vars required for full inbound flow:**
- `INBOUND_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — gates `/inbound-email` (fail-closed without it)
- `INBOUND_REPLY_TO` — Reply-To address shown to recipients (e.g., `replies@dogshow.lol`)
- A provider that routes inbound email at `replies@dogshow.lol` to a POST against `https://dogshow.schemestudio.partykit.dev/party/dogshow-live/inbound-email?secret=<INBOUND_WEBHOOK_SECRET>`. Resend Inbound is the natural pick since Resend already handles outbound — verify it's on the plan + set up MX records as Resend instructs. Postmark/CloudMailin work equivalently.

**Without inbound configured**: outbound request emails still fire after `firstAppearedAt`. Replies just land in whatever mailbox `replies@dogshow.lol` resolves to (or bounce). James can copy/paste into the admin manual-add form. The system is usable in this degraded mode — exactly how the first 3-5 testimonials will be seeded.

**Email redesign (2026-06-03, `9e0222b`)**: `sendTestimonialRequestEmail` was rebuilt CTA-first — a prominent orange "Reply with a review" button (`mailto:` link with URL-encoded subject preserving the `[ref:TOK-xxxxxxxx]` token) is now the visual focus, followed by the ask copy. The plain-reply path still works for anyone who ignores the button.

## Monthly "Dog of the Month" Season (built weekly 2026-06-11; → MONTHLY 2026-06-16, `afb1427`; public rebrand → "Dog of the Month" 2026-06-22, `d5d36d4`)

Competition-forward reframe: bones a dog earns during the calendar month (1st 00:00 → end of month 23:59 **US Eastern**) accrue to `dog.stats.seasonBones` (all-time `totalBones` untouched); the month's top dog is crowned — a permanent entry in `dog.honors[]` as `{title:'Best in Show'}` (NOT "titles", which is the *derived* badge list in `handleDogMeta`/`api/dog.js` `titlesFor()`). Revert point: tag `v1-pre-leaderboard`.

**⚠️ Naming split (after `d5d36d4`, 2026-06-22):** the **public/display** name of the race is now **"Dog of the Month"** (shown in the show.html race marquee + leaderboard label, index.html hero strip + urgency band, app.js renderers, and the 4 SEO landing pages). The **internal honor/badge term is still "Best in Show"** — `dog.honors[].title === 'Best in Show'`, and `titlesFor()` still derives `'Best in Show'` / `'N× Best in Show'`. Don't conflate the two when editing. The leaderboard section is now branded **"Hall of Bones"**; bones are surfaced as **"votes"** sitewide.

**⚠️ Weekly → monthly switch (2026-06-16, `afb1427`):** the race was originally weekly (Mon–Sun) but traffic volume was too thin to keep a weekly board populated, so it now runs per calendar month. `seasonId` changed from the Monday's date to the 1st-of-month date string; `seasonLabel()` now emits "Month of June" instead of "Week of June 8". The one-shot `/admin-migrate-monthly` endpoint seeds each community dog's `seasonBones` from its all-time `totalBones` and stamps the current month's `seasonId` so `ensureSeason()` doesn't immediately roll over on first deploy.

**Ship state (verified 2026-06-18):** `leaderboard-reframe` merged into main at `6745695`; the monthly switch (`afb1427`, 2026-06-16) is now **deployed to PartyKit prod** — live `/version` returns `afb1427` (deployedAt 2026-06-17T08:36:51Z), confirmed 2026-06-18. **The `/admin-migrate-monthly` run is still not directly confirmed** — verify `seasonBones` are seeded before trusting the monthly board isn't empty. Two earlier follow-up commits on main:
- `21eae7f` — **live race bar under the stage** (`#raceBar` in show.html, chips + placeholder lanes built in app.js `raceBar*` functions, `.race-bar*` CSS) fed by `/leaderboard` `weeklyTopDogs`.
- `12ee55a` — **"Boomer-demo" pass** (core paid audience skews 65+): `--text-dim`/`--text-faint` lightened to clear WCAG 4.5:1 (legacy hard-coded equivalents fixed across all pages + SSR templates), informational type floors raised, irony pruned from conversion surfaces ("Peek Inside" → "Watch the Show Free"), **bones glossed as "votes" at first contact** (hero, how-it-works, race subtitle), hero trust line (one-time, no subscription), cert-page share rewritten as a vote-ask ("Vote for X in this Dog Show" — share medium is family Facebook/WhatsApp).

- **seasonId** = the 1st-of-month date string (`'2026-06-01'`), computed via `Intl` in `America/New_York` (DST-safe). Helpers in `party/server.js`: `currentSeasonId()`, `seasonLabel()`, `seasonStandings()`, `ensureSeason()`.
- **Rollover is lazy**: `ensureSeason()` runs on bone accrual (`recordCommunityDogStats`), `/leaderboard` + `/dog-stats` reads, and `onAlarm` (guarantees crowning < 24h after the boundary even with zero traffic). Crowns winner → appends to `pastSeasons` storage key (capped 52) → zeroes every `seasonBones` → stores new `seasonId`.
- **/leaderboard additive fields**: `seasonId`, `seasonLabel`, `weeklyTopDogs[]` (field name kept for back-compat; now holds the *monthly* standings — `seasonBones` per dog, **real dogs only, seeds excluded**: a fake dog in a real race = fake-endorsement problem), `reigningChampion` (last `pastSeasons` winner). `topDogs` unchanged for existing consumers.
- **/dog-stats additive fields**: top-level `season` (`{id, label, rank, dogsInRace, seasonBones, leader}` — `rank: null` when the dog has 0 bones this month) and `honors[]`.
- **Surfaces**: index.html hero (top-3 race strip `#heroRace` fed by `/leaderboard`, hidden on fetch error; competition-framed hero sub-copy), show.html **race bar under the stage** (`#raceBar`, chips + placeholder lanes, `21eae7f`) + leaderboard (race section above all-time, reigning-champion line), `api/dog.js` cert page (race banner + 🏆 honor badges + vote-ask share), appearance email (rank + gap-to-next "rally your fans" block), `handleDogMeta` OG badges (Best in Show prepended).
- **Bot FACTS updated** with the monthly race (standing rule: mechanics changes MUST update `RESPONSIVE_BOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT`).
- **Anytime / fan voting (added `d5d36d4`, 2026-06-22):** votes no longer require a dog to be on stage. Shared helpers in `party/server.js` (~line 576+) `applyVotesToDog()` + `spendBonesAsVotes()` increment both `seasonBones` (this month) and `totalBones`, rolling the month over first so votes land in the right season. Two entry points: the **`voteOwnDog`** WS message (owner backs their own entry directly → `voteResult` reply) and the **`POST /vote`** HTTP endpoint (fan voting on shared `/d/{slug}` cert pages). On-stage bone-throwing still counts as voting.
- **Month-end emails + badges (added `d5d36d4`, 2026-06-22):** `sendWinnerEmail()` (~line 4225) congratulates the crowned owner on rollover; a **"race ends soon" reminder** email fires in the final stretch (the "Best in Show ends in {timeLabel}" templates ~line 4181/4194); winners get **trophy-case badges** on their cert page. **This supersedes the old "not built yet" note** below.
- Still not built (deliberate): overtake/"you've been passed" email, any prize beyond the title + badge.

## Bones-as-Currency Model (added 2026-05-26, commit `395b1e3`)

The pricing model shifted on 2026-05-26 from "tier unlocks features forever" to "registered users get a finite bones balance; paid SKUs top it up." Constants live at the top of `party/server.js`:

- `BONES_ON_REGISTER = 250` — free signup grant ⚠️ **now 50** under the 2026-06-23 free-to-enter change (`bbefb00`) — see the FREE-TO-ENTER pricing section below
- `BONES_PER_TOPUP = 250` — $1.99 "general" SKU adds this many bones (no longer unlimited)
- `BONES_LEGACY_GRANDFATHER = 2500` — one-shot grant given to pre-cutover 'general' users when the migration runs
- `BONES_TOPDOG = 1000` — $5.99 "Top Dog" (`premium_plus`) SKU adds this many bones (added 2026-06-23)
- `SLOT_DURATION_MULTIPLIER = 3` — booked Top Dog ($5.99) dogs get 3× the normal 10s on-stage time when their slot fires (slot booking is Top Dog-only; legacy $3.99 buyers keep it)

**Server-side enforcement:** the WebSocket `bone` handler reads the connection's authed user, refuses if `bones <= 0` (sends `{type:'needTopUp'}` to the sender), otherwise decrements and persists. **The legacy `tier === 'general'` unlimited-bones bypass was REMOVED 2026-06-23** — every authed user now has a finite, enforced balance (in both the `bone` handler and `spendVote`). `/admin-migrate-general` had already converted those legacy accounts to `tier='free'` with `bones = max(current, 2500)`, `paidSku='general'` retained.

**Client-side:** `app.js` listens for `boneBalance` (sync) and `needTopUp` (out-of-bones) WebSocket messages and updates the on-screen bone pill. `/my-dog` and `/get-user` both return the authoritative `bones` field so the show page can render the balance immediately on load without waiting for the first WebSocket message.

## Slot Booking + Pre-Show Pages (added 2026-05-26, commit `395b1e3`)

Top Dog ($5.99) buyers — and legacy $3.99 `premium` buyers, who keep the perk — can choose **"Show me right away"** or **"Schedule it for later"** during upload. Free / Dog Entry Pro uploaders skip the slot picker entirely (the server rejects `slotAt` from non-Top-Dog with `slot_requires_topdog`). A scheduled slot is persisted as `dog.slotAt` (ms timestamp). When a slot fires, the server interrupts the current rotation to put the booked dog on stage for `SLOT_DURATION_MULTIPLIER × baseDuration`. Slots that miss their grace window are skipped (the dog re-enters the normal rotation).

Per-dog certificate pages at `/d/{slug}` are now **state-aware** (rendered by `api/dog.js`):

- `firstAppearedAt === null` → **pre-show**: countdown + RSVP form. Fans drop an email and get a 1hr + 5min reminder before the slot fires. `POST /rsvp` registers them as `tier='free'` with 50 bones, records `{email, userId, rsvpAt, sent1h:false, sent5m:false}` on the dog, and re-arms the storage alarm.
- `firstAppearedAt !== null` → **post-show certificate** (the existing certificate flow — titles, stats, share buttons, more dogs).

The daily reconciliation alarm was generalized into `computeNextWakeupAt()` + `scheduleNextWakeup()` so the same alarm now drives both the 24h audit AND the per-RSVP T-60min / T-5min reminder emails (`processSlotReminders()`).

## Responsive Chat Bot (added 2026-05-27)

One bot in the chat room — `sir_barks_alot` — can react to real user messages via a **Claude Haiku 4.5** call (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`). The other 14 bots in `BOTS` (`party/server.js`) remain fully canned (fixed message pool, cycled by the existing scheduler). The responsive path only fires when a real user posts a chat message and a stack of gates passes; SKIP convention lets the model stay silent for off-topic / hostile / injection attempts.

**Feature gate:** OFF unless `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set in PartyKit env (`npx partykit env add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from the `party/` dir). Without the key, sir_barks_alot behaves exactly like the other canned bots.

**Always-present seeding:** when LLM mode is on, `startBot()` force-seeds sir_barks_alot into `activeBots` before any other seeding runs, and the random join/leave randomizer filters him out of leave candidates. Without LLM mode, he obeys the same random join/leave as every other bot. Helper: `responsiveBotEnabled()`.

**Trigger conditions** (gates in order, cheapest first — all live at the top of `party/server.js`):
1. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` present (kill switch by absence)
2. No call in-flight for this room (`barksInflight` mutex)
3. UX cooldown — `RESPONSIVE_BOT_MIN_REPLY_GAP_MS` (6s) since his last reply
4. Probabilistic roll — `RESPONSIVE_BOT_REPLY_PROB` (40%)
5. Per-room hourly cap — `RESPONSIVE_BOT_MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR` (60), sliding window on `barksApiCallTimestamps`
6. Recent context must contain ≥1 non-bot message (prevents bot-to-bot loops)

**API call:** last `RESPONSIVE_BOT_CONTEXT_WINDOW` (8) chat messages formatted as `user: text` lines, sent as the single user-turn payload with `RESPONSIVE_BOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` as system. `max_tokens: 60` (sized for the 2–20 word reply range). Output is run through `this.sanitize()` and capped at 200 chars, same path as user chat. If the model outputs `SKIP` (any case, with trailing punctuation), nothing is broadcast.

**Persona prompt** locks the bot to a warm, charming English-country-manor-host voice (Wodehouse-genial, not snobbish-critic) at 2–20 word fragments, framed as a fellow power-user viewer rather than a staffer. Three rule blocks: (1) **content guardrails** — no slurs, no profanity, no insult/flame/rant; non-positive sentiment allowed only when phrased with warmth ("not my favorite, but charming") or else SKIP; (2) **character guardrails** — standard jailbreak resistance (ignore-prior-instructions, persona-change, model-reveal, recite-poem, switch-language, no-bot-disclosure all explicit), plus an "you are not a Dog Show employee" rule; (3) **product talk** — hard no on unprompted marketing/pitching/upgrade-talk, narrow permission to answer DIRECT product questions briefly in character using a small embedded **FACTS YOU KNOW AS A REGULAR** list (post-2026-06-23 free-to-enter: entry is free; prices $1.99/$5.99; bones: 50 on register, 250 per $1.99 top-up, 1000 for the $5.99 Top Dog, ~500ms per bone capped at 15s bonus/dog; rotation: ~10s base, ~30s for Top Dog booked slots; one dog per account). Anything outside that list, the bot is instructed to defer in character or SKIP rather than invent. Not defended in prompt: encoded payloads (base64/leetspeak) and indirect framing attacks; relies on Haiku's training there. Blast radius is small — only output channel is a chat message ≤200 chars.

> **⚠️ Maintenance burden:** the FACTS block in `RESPONSIVE_BOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` hardcodes prices and mechanics. **Whenever you change a price, the bones-per-X math, the rotation duration, the slot multiplier, or the one-dog-per-account rule, you MUST also update this prompt.** The bot will otherwise tell users wrong numbers with full confidence. Premium-plus bones figure is intentionally left vague in the prompt because the implementation ("floor to 1000") doesn't match the marketing copy ("2× launch bonus") — fix the alignment before hardcoding a number.

**Cost ceiling:** worst case 60 calls/hour/room × ~$0.0005/call ≈ $0.03/hour/room. Realistic with the 30% trigger + 8s cooldown is closer to $0.003–0.01/hour/room.

**Failure handling:** API errors are caught in `maybeBarksReply`, logged, reported to Sentry via `reportToSentry()`, and the user's original chat is unaffected — the LLM call is fire-and-forget from the chat handler.

## Cookie Consent (GDPR / ePrivacy) — added 2026-06-04

Non-essential trackers no longer fire on page load. They're gated behind explicit consent for visitors in scope of GDPR/UK-GDPR, and load by default elsewhere.

**Buckets:**
- **Essential (always loads):** Sentry browser error tracking. In-memory, sets no cookies/device IDs, so it's outside ePrivacy cookie-consent; justified as legitimate-interest stability/security and disclosed in `privacy.html` §3/§4. Keep PII scrubbing on (don't enable `sendDefaultPii`).
- **Non-essential (consent-gated):** Google Analytics 4, Faurya, Bing UET, Microsoft Clarity (Clarity still also gated by a blank `CLARITY_ID`).

**Geo scope (EU-only):** `middleware.ts` classifies each request via Vercel geolocation and sets `dogshow_geo=EU|ROW`. EEA + UK + Switzerland → `EU` → banner shown, trackers gated. Everyone else → `ROW` → trackers load with no banner (US, etc. — opt-out regimes). Decision was deliberate (James, 2026-06-04): preserve US ad-conversion data while complying where opt-in is required. **Fail-safe:** missing/unknown geo is treated as `EU`.

**Flow:**
1. Every page loads `consent.js` **before** `analytics.js` (both `defer`).
2. `consent.js` exposes `window.dogshowConsent`; `analytics.js` calls `.onGrant(loadGatedTrackers)`. The callback fires immediately if consent is already granted (returning accepter) or implicitly granted (ROW), else when the visitor clicks Accept.
3. Precedence in `consent.js` `init()`: explicit stored choice (`granted`/`denied`) wins everywhere → else `ROW` loads trackers (no persist) → else show banner.
4. Withdraw/change: footer "Cookie settings" links + the privacy-policy link carry `data-cookie-settings`, wired to `dogshowConsent.reopen()`.

**Storage:** `dogshow_consent` (`granted`/`denied`) + `dogshow_consent_at` (ISO) in localStorage; `dogshow_geo` cookie (set server-side). All three are strictly-necessary and disclosed in `privacy.html` §4.

> **⚠️ Maintenance:** if you add a new tracker/pixel, wire it inside `loadGatedTrackers()` in `analytics.js` (gated) or alongside Sentry (essential) — never inline in a page `<head>`, or it bypasses consent. New cookies must be added to the `privacy.html` §4 table.

> **Known limits / follow-ups:** (a) Geo only resolves on deployed Vercel requests — verify the `dogshow_geo` cookie + `ROW`/`EU` values in DevTools after deploy. (b) ROW users who later Reject via Cookie settings only stop trackers on the *next* page load (can't unload a running script). (c) CCPA strictly wants an explicit "Do Not Sell/Share" opt-out link for US visitors — the Cookie-settings reject covers the spirit; a dedicated link is a possible future addition.

PartyKit WebSocket, dog slideshow sync, live chat, bone reactions, **race bar renderer** (`raceBar*` functions ~line 1153 — monthly-race chips from `/leaderboard` `weeklyTopDogs` (field name unchanged), placeholder lanes when fewer than 3 real dogs, `21eae7f`), community upload (canvas resize 600px JPEG 0.7) via shared `submitDogImage()` — also auto-submits a pre-purchase photo stashed in `localStorage` (incl. optional `dogshow_pending_dog_slot`) on show entry. **UX-audit M3 fix (2026-06-04)**: the stashed pending-dog photo is now cleared only after `/upload-dog` actually succeeds (not on read), so a premium buyer who abandons the "Pick a name" username modal no longer ends up a paid-but-no-dog account — the upload retries on next show entry. breed facts DB, leaderboard, share rail. **Frenzy is pure-visual:** `updateStreak()` only toggles the `.active` class via a 3 bps → on / 1.5 bps → off hysteresis; `playFrenzyVoice()` plays `/sounds/bone-frenzy.m4a` on each trigger (browser speech synthesis fallback if the audio file fails). **Inline register modal** (`showRegisterModal(context)`, 2026-05-26) replaces the old upgrade modal for unregistered users — context `'bone'` vs `'chat'` swaps the title/subtitle. **Bones balance:** listens for `boneBalance` (sync) and `needTopUp` (out-of-bones) WebSocket messages and shows a top-up modal when empty. **House rotator** (multi-message, rotating; free entry CTA + $1.99 bones top-up card + fake doors including the "funny music video of your dog" card added 2026-05-28 `69c28e0`). Each card can carry `excludeForTiers` and `requiresRegistered` flags — bones-top-up card hidden for unregistered users. **Interest modal** captures fake-door emails via `/register` with `tier='interest_<feature>'`. Breed dropdown options come from `window.DOG_BREEDS` (breeds.js). **Free-to-enter (2026-06-23):** the in-show upload UI is now shown to any registered user (was premium-only); a prominent "➕ Get bones" button (`#getBonesBtn`) sits beside Give-a-bone and opens the top-up modal; the bones pill enters a low-balance warning state at ≤10; `slotAt` is only sent for Top Dog (gated by `window.canBookSlot`); register-grant copy is 50 bones. || `style.css` | ~3001 | Shared stylesheet. | `analytics.js` | ~180 | Sitewide tracker loader. **Sentry browser loader loads unconditionally** (essential — in-memory, no cookies, legitimate-interest). **GA + Faurya + Bing UET + Clarity are consent-gated** (2026-06-04): they're injected by a `loadGatedTrackers()` that only runs via `window.dogshowConsent.onGrant()` — i.e. after the visitor accepts (or implicitly, for non-EU visitors; see consent section). The old inline GA bootstrap + Faurya `<script>` were removed from every page. Exports `window.trackEmailCapture` / `window.trackPurchase` (no-op until trackers load; all call sites already guard with `if (window.gtag)` etc.). |
| `consent.js` | ~210 | **Cookie consent gate** (added 2026-06-04). Self-contained banner (injected CSS, brand colors, Accept/Reject equally prominent, Reject first in DOM), localStorage state (`dogshow_consent` = `granted`/`denied` + `dogshow_consent_at`), and the `window.dogshowConsent` API (`grant`/`deny`/`reopen`/`onGrant`/`status`/`granted`). **EU-only gating:** reads the `dogshow_geo` cookie set by `middleware.ts` — `ROW` + no stored choice → trackers load with no banner; `EU` or missing/unknown (fail-safe) → banner shown; an explicit stored choice always wins. Loaded **before** `analytics.js` on every page. Wires any `[data-cookie-settings]` element (and the footer "Cookie settings" links) to `reopen()`. |

Audit #48 fix — owner picks the breed themselves, so the AI can never mislabel a dog. "Mixed Breed" pinned first, "Other / Not sure" last. **Extended 2026-05-29** with ~25 designer-mix + size-variant breeds (Bernedoodle, Goldendoodle, Cockapoo, Mini Aussie, Mini Golden Retriever, Saint Berdoodle, etc.) to give SEO breed-hub pages real user-upload coverage. Resolved follow-ups render in a collapsed `<details>` pane beneath the active list — Zendesk-style ticket history kept forever in storage. Auth = `ADMIN_KEY` typed into the login box (not stored server-side beyond the env var). `meta robots = noindex,nofollow`; also Disallowed in `robots.txt`. |

Corrected to the real shapes: `/landing-stats` → `totalBones` / `totalFans` / `totalDogs` / `watching`; `/leaderboard` → `topDogs[]` (fallback `recent`/`top`) with per-dog `dogName` + `totalBones`. **When adding live widgets to any page, consume these exact field names** — the bare `bones`/`fans`/`dogs`/`name`/`top` keys do not exist.

Tier-1 scope of ~30 breed-info targets (~500K combined volume); requires expanding `breeds.js` to cover designer-mix breeds (Bernedoodle, Cavapoo, etc.) before the unique-value pitch holds. Largely shipped now — 19 breed pages live (see `api/breed.js`). |
| `90-day-growth-trial-plan.md` | **Planning doc (added 2026-06-05, ~177 lines).** Governs the two parallel acquisition bets — paid search (Bing + Google Ads) and the `/breeds/{slug}` SEO hub — on separate go/no-go yardsticks. Core thesis: paid search is a learning lab, not a profit center (the $3.99 one-time SKU caps ROAS at a ~$3.57 breakeven CPA); SEO is the structurally-aligned compounding bet but 90 days only shows leading indicators (indexation, impressions, avg position), not revenue. Includes unit-economics table, kill criteria. Pulls from `bing-campaign-prep.md`, `bing-launch-checklist.md`, `seo-breed-hub-plan.md`, `google-ads-keywords.csv`. |
| `free-to-enter-migration-2026-06-23.md` | **Change-set + deploy/prod-action checklist (added 2026-06-23, `bbefb00`)** for the free-to-enter pivot — entry made free, money only buys bones, new 4-tier structure, `BONES_ON_REGISTER` 250→50, `BONES_TOPDOG=1000`, `/admin-grant-goodwill` for legacy $3.99 buyers. Source of truth for the migration; see the FREE-TO-ENTER pricing section. |
| `breed-outreach-list-and-template.md` | **Outreach working doc (added 2026-06-23, `bbefb00`; edited since).** Breed-community outreach target list + message template. Marketing ops, not site code (`.vercelignore`d via `*.md`). |
| `outreach-staging-log.md` | Staging/tracking notes for breed-community outreach sends (added 2026-06-23; **now committed + updated by the `breed-outreach.yml` GH Action**). Marketing ops, `.vercelignore`d. |
| `free-to-enter-tracking.csv` | Metrics tracking for the free-to-enter pivot (added 2026-06-23; **now committed + snapshotted by the `free-to-enter-tracker.yml` GH Action**). `.vercelignore`d (`*.csv`). || `sounds/` | Audio assets. `bone-frenzy.m4a` — voice clip played by `app.js` when frenzy triggers (added 2026-05-28 `8651797`). |
| `UX-AUDIT-2026-06-04.md` | **UX/flow audit (2026-06-04)** — live walkthrough of production + real emails inspected in Gmail, cross-referenced against the repo. Real $3.99 BYD purchase tested end-to-end. Findings: 1 High open at audit time / 1 High found-and-fixed (H1 unsubscribe), 2 Medium (M1 geo middleware `.mjs`→`.ts`, M2 success-page silent failure, M3 stuck-paid-user upload gate), ~9 Low. Most are now fixed (see commits `180c130`→`db4fb38`). Central theme: **PartyKit prod can silently drift from the repo** — drove the new `/version` + `check-deploy` tooling. Lists test accounts/data created for cleanup. |
| `accessibility-audit-2026-06-17.md` | **A11y audit (2026-06-17, shipped `cb89878` 2026-06-18)** — WCAG 2.2 AA pass over the live static site (audience skews 65+, so focus visibility/target size/contrast weighted heavily). **Applied (additive, non-breaking):** global `:focus-visible` ring, `.sr-only`/`.skip-link` utilities + `prefers-reduced-motion` in `style.css`; skip links, `sr-only` input labels, `aria-modal`/`role="dialog"` markup, FAQ `aria-expanded`, chat `role="log"`/`aria-live`, login `role="status"` across index/show/dogs/login. **Recommended but NOT applied (open follow-ups):** R1 modal focus-trapping + Escape-to-close + focus restore (High — the `aria-modal` markup is only honest once this lands), R2 missing `<h1>` on about/dogs, R3 target-size for 65+ audience, R4 dynamic alt on the rotating stage image, R5 unverified contrast on remaining inline colors (esp. `#3d2d6b`), R6 SSR templates (`api/dog.js`/`api/breed.js`) still need per-control labels + skip links. |
| `google-ads-keywords.csv` | Google Ads keyword list (added 2026-06-04, `2620b7b`) — companion to the Bing prep docs for the search-ads trial. |
| `google-ads-negative-keywords.txt` | Google Ads negative-keyword list (added 2026-06-04, `2620b7b`). |
| `platform-audit.md` | Pre-paid-traffic reliability audit (generated 2026-05-19). 6 Critical / 7 High / 6 Medium / 3 Low findings — silent failure modes, 2 security gaps allowing free tier upgrades, near-zero observability. The 6 Critical fixes shipped 2026-05-21 (`535deb6`); subsequent commits cleared more findings: #37 pre-purchase photo (`3cbe942`), High-2 rate limiting + crypto tokens + magic-link UX + welcome email (`7ecf996`), #40 admin CMS (`3404ab7`), #38 AI classifier (`15e64ef`), #48 user-selected breed (`e26ca57`), High-3 Sentry (`08bd136`), #27 per-dog SSR via Vercel (`a8ce1b0` + `c6185a6`), and #37/Anonymous/cert-email/stale-image cleanup (`086b359`). Later commits (post-audit, 2026-05-25 → 2026-05-28): OG-image font + per-dog dynamic OG + bridge-interstitial removal (`b6dddd8`), Vercel install/output/tsconfig fixes (`dc89bc5`, `e0a3e52`, `ced94f6`), bones-as-currency + slot booking + pre-show cert pages (`395b1e3`), rotator card visibility for unregistered users (`f1957d5`), frenzy UI stripped to pure-visual (`b97ed01`), modal scrolls on short viewports + softened post-purchase email copy (`3f0aa22`), CAN-SPAM physical address in email footer (`543d478`), BONE FRENZY badge + bone-frenzy.m4a sound + unsubscribe footer (`8651797`), funny-video fake-door card (`69c28e0`), UET tag wired live (`1621f41`), Privacy + Terms pages + sitewide footer (`9a0e8cd`, closes CAN-SPAM / app-store legal-requirement gap), SEO breed hub P0 + Bernedoodle pilot (`47107b8`), 5 more breed pages — Goldendoodle, Mini Golden Retriever, Saint Berdoodle, Mini Aussie, Pomsky (`bebc51e`). 2026-06-01: real testimonials reply-by-email + admin curation (`e7ab7ed`), ticker driven by `/testimonials` with fake quotes removed (`0a51bca`), Scheme Studio links unified on `www.schemestudio.lol` across about/index/privacy/terms (`2e3613f`). 2026-06-02: landing-page section reorder + raise testimonial gate from 1 → 3 approved (`50fbe34`). 2026-06-03: testimonial-request email rebuilt CTA-first + stuck-paid-user resolve/unresolve flow with `stuckResolutions` storage + admin weekly summary throttle (`9e0222b`). |
| `CNAME` | Domain: `dogshow.lol` |
| `vercel.json` | Vercel rewrites: `/d/:slug` → `/api/dog?slug=:slug` (SSR), `/breeds/:slug` → `/api/breed?slug=:slug` (SSR, added 2026-05-29), and `/:slug` → `/:slug.html` (clean URLs — also serves `/breeds` → `breeds.html`). Order matters — specific routes before the catch-all. Also sets `outputDirectory: "."` so Vercel doesn't expect a `public/` build dir (`e0a3e52`). |
| `.vercelignore` | Excludes `party/`, `*.md`, `*.docx`, `*.py`, `DogShowPrototype.jsx`, `preview-redesign.html`, `preview-pale.html`, and (added `77e67cd`) `scripts`, `*.csv`, `prompts`, `.github`, `data` from the Vercel deployment (also closes the prior GH-Pages leak of internal docs). |
| `.github/workflows/` | **GitHub Actions cloud automations (added 2026-07-13, `77e67cd`)** — migrated off James's local scheduled Mac tasks (2026-07-03). Four workflows: `wiki-update.yml` (weekly CLAUDE.md maintenance — this task), `free-to-enter-tracker.yml` (metrics snapshot), `breed-outreach.yml` (breed-community outreach drafts), `ads-search1-report.yml` (ads reporting). Each pairs with a prompt in `prompts/` and/or a script in `scripts/`. Report out to James via Telegram (`scripts/notify_telegram.sh`). |
| `prompts/` | **Added 2026-07-13 (`77e67cd`).** Prompt files driving the GitHub Actions automations: `wiki-update.md`, `breed-outreach.md`, `ads-search1-report.md`. |
| `scripts/` | **Added 2026-07-13 (`77e67cd`) + `0d110b9`.** Automation/utility scripts: `notify_telegram.sh`, `track_free_to_enter.py`, `gmail_create_drafts.py`, `ads-search1-google-ads-script.js` (in-Google-Ads script), plus the breed-hero generator `generate-breed-heroes.mjs` + `breed-hero-prompts.json` (Gemini `gemini-3.1-flash-image`, `0d110b9`). `.vercelignore`d. |
| `breeds-img/` | **Added 2026-06-19 (`0d110b9`).** 19 photoreal AI breed hero images (one per live `/breeds/{slug}`), generated with Gemini. Used as the breed-page hero fallback when no real submitted dog of that breed exists. || `tsconfig.json` | TypeScript/JSX compiler options for `api/og.tsx` (added `ced94f6` so Vercel can compile the Edge function's JSX). `include: ["api/**/*"]`. |
| `_redirects` | Legacy Netlify/Vercel redirect file — superseded by `vercel.json`. Kept in repo but not the source of truth. |
| `robots.txt` | SEO crawl rules. Disallows `/admin` + `/admin.html`. `Sitemap:` pointer + a non-standard `LLM: https://dogshow.lol/llms.txt` pointer (added 2026-06-18). |
| `sitemap.xml` | Static sitemap (~143 lines; regenerate via `generate-sitemap.html`). Includes main pages + 4 SEO landing pages + `/breeds` index + the 19 live `/breeds/{slug}` hub pages. |
| `llms.txt` | **GEO / AI-crawler guide (added 2026-06-18).** Spec-format markdown at site root (H1 + summary blockquote → key facts → curated link sections: main pages, the 19 breed guides, enter-your-dog + 4 contest pages, `## Optional` legal). Served at `https://dogshow.lol/llms.txt` (`.txt` deploys — not in `.vercelignore`; Vercel serves root static files ahead of the `/:slug` rewrite, same as `robots.txt`/`sitemap.xml`). Pointed at from `robots.txt`. **Reality check:** llms.txt is crawled but unproven as a citation/ranking signal — Google (Illyes/Mueller, 2025) explicitly doesn't support it; treat as cheap hygiene, not a fix for the AI-citation gap. **⚠️ SYNC — this file hardcodes site-wide facts (prices $1.99/$3.99/$5.99, 250-bones grant, "votes" gloss, the 19 breed slugs, monthly Best in Show).** It belongs to the same hardcoded-facts set as `RESPONSIVE_BOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (`party/server.js`) and `brand-voice.md`: whenever you change a price, the bones math, the breed list, or a core mechanic, update `llms.txt`, the bot prompt, AND `brand-voice.md` together — these are all site-wide and silently go stale otherwise. |
| `YangBagus.ttf` | Custom font |
| `og-image.png` | Open Graph / Twitter Card share image (1200x630). Generated 2026-05-18 with Python+PIL via `make-og-image.py` — wordmark in Yang Bagus + theatre proscenium framing. Referenced by all main HTML pages. Swap with a designer version anytime — no code change required. |
| `make-og-image.py` | Python+PIL generator script for `og-image.png`. Run `python3 make-og-image.py` from repo root to regenerate. Only "The Dog Show" wordmark uses Yang Bagus; supporting text falls back to DejaVu Sans for portability. Per-dog share previews use `api/og.tsx` (Edge function) instead — same brand language, different generator. |
| `dogshow-promo-video.mp4` + `dogshow-promo-poster.jpg` | **Added 2026-06-16 (`afb1427`).** Autoplay/muted/loop promo video + its poster frame, embedded in the `index.html` hero (`.lp-promo-video-wrap`). ~4.5MB mp4. |
| `favicon.svg` + PNG variants | Favicons |
| `party/` | PartyKit server code — single `server.js` (~5271 lines, one class), has its own node_modules. NOT deployed to Vercel (`.vercelignore`). Growth since 2026-05-29 driven by the testimonial reply-by-email + admin curation flow (`e7ab7ed`), the stuck-user resolve flow + weekly summary throttle (`9e0222b`), the 2026-06-04 UX-audit fixes (L4 backfill-slugs endpoint, `/version` deploy stamp, L8 scheduled-cert email, L9 duplicate-reminder fix), the monthly-race + anytime-voting work (`d5d36d4`), and the free-to-enter pivot (`bbefb00`). **128KB DO-storage fix (2026-06-25, `0999911`):** PartyKit/Durable-Objects caps any stored value at 131072 bytes; `/upload-dog` now rejects `imageData > 125000` bytes with `image_too_large` before `put()`, and `app.js` `compressDogImage()` (+ index.html's pre-purchase path) caps BOTH dimensions and steps quality/size down. **Onboarding cadence overhaul (2026-07-08, `2d6451b`):** the inline welcome email was removed from register/RSVP; a new user now gets exactly ONE deferred welcome from `processEmailCampaigns()` after `WELCOME_GRACE_MS` (20min, lets an owner's upload preempt the fan welcome), then `EMAIL_QUIET_MS` (7 days) suppresses ALL campaign/testimonial email (transactional mail — magic link, purchase confirmation, slot reminders — is exempt); `WELCOME_MAX_AGE` (3 days) backfills pre-existing accounts silently. Cert email gained a votes CTA. |
| `party/build-info.js` | **Added 2026-06-04.** Exports `BUILD_INFO` placeholder (`{commit:'dev', dirty:false, deployedAt:null}`), overwritten at deploy time by `stamp-version.cjs` and served at `GET /version`. Do NOT edit by hand. |
| `party/stamp-version.cjs` | **Added 2026-06-04.** Run by `npm run deploy` — stamps the real git commit + dirty flag + timestamp into `build-info.js` so each deploy bakes its version into the bundle. |
| `party/check-deploy.cjs` | **Added 2026-06-04.** `npm run check-deploy` — compares live prod `GET /version` against local `git HEAD`, prints in-sync / drifted. Catches PartyKit prod↔repo drift (UX-audit H1/H2 root cause). |
| `api/dog.js` | Vercel serverless function (~565 lines). Server-renders `/d/{slug}` with two states: **pre-show** (countdown + RSVP form for dogs with `firstAppearedAt === null`) and **post-show certificate** (existing flow with titles, stats, share, more dogs). Fetches dog data from PartyKit (`/dog-meta`, `/dog-stats`). Sitewide footer (Home/About/Privacy/Terms) added 2026-05-29 (`9a0e8cd`). Resolves audit #27. **UX-audit fixes (2026-06-04)**: L10 — RSVP confirmation copy softened to "we'll email a reminder before they take the stage" (no longer promises a "1 hour before" reminder that can't fire for near-term slots); L12 — the RSVP-issued session token now only adopts for a logged-*out* visitor (`!localStorage.dogshow_token`), so a logged-in owner who RSVPs to another dog isn't silently swapped off their own account. |
| `api/breed.js` | **Vercel serverless function** (added 2026-05-29, ~1066 lines). Server-renders `/breeds/{slug}` breed hub pages. Template anatomy: lede → live-show widget → spotlight (show-ring lens) → facts dl → owner-fit → famous → bonus user-dogs section (hidden when N=0, fetched from PartyKit `/dogs-by-breed`) → CTA → related breeds → footer. Content embedded in the `BREEDS` object — **19 breeds written, all committed + live on prod** (`9a6f2e3`, deployed 2026-06-11; verified 2026-06-12). ~1050 visible words each: bernedoodle, goldendoodle, mini-golden-retriever, saint-berdoodle, mini-aussie, pomsky (the original 6 from `bebc51e`), plus australian-labradoodle, teacup-poodle, mini-dachshund, german-shepherd, golden-mountain-dog, toy-aussie, french-bulldog, cockapoo, labradoodle, maltipoo, cavapoo, cane-corso, dalmatian. Bernedoodle is the voice/structure template; clone for any further breeds. `renderRelatedHtml()` emits an `<a>` for related breeds whose slug exists in `BREEDS` and a muted "Soon" `<span>` chip otherwise — prevents internal 404s from inter-breed links. Schema.org `Article` + `BreadcrumbList`. Brand voice throughout — see [[feedback-seo-pages-brand-voice]]. Pages must work without user dogs of that breed — see [[feedback-breed-pages-no-user-dog-dependency]]. **Top-of-funnel redesign (2026-06-19, `0d110b9`):** sections reordered so search intent (facts + owner-fit) comes above the show-bridge; reader-first "See {plural} on the live show" heading + free-watch CTA replaced the company-voice spotlight; dual bottom CTA (primary "Watch the show free" + owner CTA → `/?openModal=premium`); a **hero image between H1 and lede** — a real submitted dog of the breed (linked to its cert) when one exists, else the AI breed photo in `breeds-img/`, else a "Be the first {Breed}" prompt. || `api/og.tsx` | Vercel Edge function (~203 lines). Generates a 1200x630 (1.91:1 — Facebook/Twitter/LinkedIn optimal) per-dog share image at `/api/og?slug={slug}`. Composites the dog's photo into a branded frame so share previews don't get cropped weirdly. `api/dog.js` sets `og:image` / `twitter:image` to point at this. Uses `@vercel/og` (declared in `package.json`). |
| `package.json` | Declares Vercel function dependencies (`@vercel/functions` — added 2026-06-04 for the geo middleware; `@vercel/og`, `react`). Includes a no-op `build` script — needed because without one, Vercel sometimes skips `npm install`, which leaves the Edge functions unable to resolve their deps (`dc89bc5`). **Stays CommonJS — do NOT add `"type":"module"`** (would break the CJS `api/dog.js`/`api/breed.js`); the middleware is `middleware.ts` (compiled by Vercel, ESM imports, no package.json change). |
| `middleware.ts` | **Vercel Edge Middleware** (added 2026-06-04). Reads `geolocation(request).country` from `@vercel/functions` and sets a strictly-necessary `dogshow_geo=EU\|ROW` cookie (1-day, `SameSite=Lax; Secure`) on page responses, consumed by `consent.js`. `EU` = EEA-27 + IS/LI/NO + GB + CH; everything else = `ROW`; unknown country → `EU` (fail-safe). `matcher` excludes `/api/*` and any extensioned path (static assets), so it runs only on document routes incl. `/d/:slug` + `/breeds/:slug`. **Must be `.ts`/`.js`, NOT `.mjs`** — Vercel only detects middleware by extensions ts/tsx/js/jsx; a `middleware.mjs` under `outputDirectory:"."` is silently served as a static file and never runs (this exact bug shipped 2026-06-04 and was caught by checking `/middleware.mjs` returned 200). Added to `tsconfig.json` `include`. **Geo headers are only populated on deployed Vercel requests — locally everyone gets the banner (fail-safe), which is expected.** |

## API Endpoints (PartyKit)

Base: `dogshow.schemestudio.partykit.dev/party/dogshow-live` (PartyKit single-party default; app.js uses `/party/{room}`, not `/parties/{name}/{room}`)

> **Rate limiting (audit High-2):** Per-IP sliding-window limiter on the abuse-prone POST endpoints — `register` 5/min, `login` 5/min, `create-checkout` 5/min, `verify-checkout` 10/min, `upload-dog` 5/min. Excess returns HTTP 429. Defined inline at the top of the dispatcher in `party/server.js`.

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | `/landing-stats` | Stats for landing page (bones, fans, dogs, watching) |
| POST | `/register` | Register a free signup or interest-capture lead → returns token. **Rejects `general`/`premium` (403)** — paid tiers must go through `/verify-checkout`. Sends a welcome email via Resend on first signup (audit hardening, `7ecf996`). |
| POST | `/login` | **Magic-link request** (renamed in behavior). Takes `{email}`, generates a 60-min `magic:<token>` storage entry, emails the user a `login.html?token=…` link via Resend. Always returns a generic success message (does not leak whether the email is registered). |
| POST | `/verify` | Verify a session token OR consume a magic-link token. Magic tokens are single-use with a short 2-min grace window for double-clicks; on success it issues a 30-day session token. |
| POST | `/get-user` | Get user data by token |
| POST | `/my-dog` | Returns the caller's real (server-side) tier, their dog `{id,slug,dogName}` if any, and the authoritative `bones` balance. The show page calls this on entry so a returning premium user who already uploaded sees a "view your certificate" link, a refunded/free account doesn't see the upload UI, and the bones pill can render immediately. |
| POST | `/set-username` | Set username for current user |
| POST | `/create-checkout` | Create Stripe checkout session. `success_url` carries `{CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}` + `metadata[tier]`. Two valid tiers (2026-06-23): `general` ($1.99, +250 bones) and `premium_plus` ($5.99 Top Dog, +1000 bones + slot/3×). The `premium` $3.99 SKU is retired. Price IDs are hard-coded in `priceMap`. |
| POST | `/verify-checkout` | Verify a completed Stripe Checkout Session server-side (payment_status=paid), then provision the paid user. **Only path that can grant general/premium/premium_plus.** tier+email read from Stripe, never the client. |
| POST | `/rsvp` | Phase 4 pre-show pages. Body: `{email, dogId, slug?}`. Validates the dog exists + is still pre-air, auto-registers the email as a free user (50 bones), records `{email, userId, rsvpAt, sent1h:false, sent5m:false}` on the dog, and re-arms the storage alarm. Returns the user's session token + bones so the client can transition into the show. Special return shapes: `alreadyAired: true` (dog has aired), `airingNow: true` (slot passed but within grace window). |
| POST | `/vote` | **Added 2026-06-22 (`d5d36d4`).** Fan voting on shared `/d/{slug}` cert pages — casts `count` bone-votes for a specific dog independent of who's on stage. Backed by `spendBonesAsVotes()`/`applyVotesToDog()`; increments both `seasonBones` (rolls the month over first) and `totalBones`. WS sibling: `voteOwnDog` (owner backs own entry). |
| POST | `/stripe-webhook` | Stripe webhook. HMAC-verified via `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Handles `charge.refunded` (→ downgrade to free) + `charge.dispute.created` (→ flag user). |
| POST | `/upload-dog` | Upload community dog (**any registered user — 2026-06-23, entry is free; was premium-only**). Optional `slotAt` is accepted **only from Top Dog buyers** (`paidSku === 'premium_plus'`, or legacy `'premium'`); a `slotAt` from anyone else is rejected with `slot_requires_topdog`. The booked slot triggers precision interruption of the rotation when due, with 3× duration. Runs AI dog classification via the **Cloudflare AI REST API** (`@cf/microsoft/resnet-50`, fix `15e64ef` — replaced the previous Workers AI binding that wasn't bound in production): rejects non-dogs, else records confidence. **Fails open** — accepts as "Mystery Breed" if the AI errors or is unavailable. Breed comes from the uploader's dropdown pick (#48); `username` is passed so the dog is never created "Anonymous". **One dog per account** — a 2nd upload returns 409 `already_have_dog` with the existing dog's slug; bypassable by passing `adminKey` (=`ADMIN_KEY`). **Rejects `imageData` >125000 bytes with `image_too_large`** before storing (`0999911`, 2026-06-25 — PartyKit/Durable-Objects caps any stored value at 131072 bytes; oversized data URLs threw RangeError). Sends the certificate email (dog photo + `/d/{slug}` link) on success. Error responses include a `code` field for client-side observability. |
| GET | `/community-image?id=X` | Fetch uploaded community dog image |
| GET | `/community-count` | Count of community-uploaded dogs |
| GET | `/dog-stats?id=X` | Individual dog stats |
| GET | `/dog-meta?slug=X` | Per-dog OG/meta tags for share crawlers |
| GET | `/show-meta` | OG/meta tags for show share rail |
| GET | `/resolve-slug?slug=X` | Resolve slug to dog ID |
| GET | `/all-dogs` | All dogs for gallery |
| GET | `/dogs-by-breed?breed=X[&limit=Y]` | **Added 2026-05-29; verified live in prod 2026-06-11.** Returns dogs whose `breed` matches (case-insensitive) and have aired (`firstAppearedAt` set), sorted by `totalBones` desc, capped at min(limit, 24), default 12. Consumed by `api/breed.js` for the bonus user-dogs section on `/breeds/{slug}` pages. |
| GET | `/leaderboard` | Top dogs + recent arrivals |
| GET | `/admin-audit?key=X` | Admin audit — tier counts, **active** stuck premium users (paid but no dog entry, minus anything in `stuckResolutions`), `resolvedPremium` history, and orphaned `img:`/`slug:` storage keys. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. Logic shared with the reconciliation alarm via `computeAudit()`. Consumed by `admin.html`. |
| POST | `/admin-resolve-stuck` | **Added 2026-06-03 (`9e0222b`).** Body `{key, userId, note?}`. Marks a stuck paid user as handled — drops them from the active worklist + future weekly summary emails. Resolution row is kept forever in `stuckResolutions` (Zendesk-style ticket close). Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |
| POST | `/admin-unresolve-stuck` | **Added 2026-06-03 (`9e0222b`).** Body `{key, userId}`. Reverses a resolution — removes the row from `stuckResolutions` so the user reappears in the active list on next audit. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |
| GET | `/admin-delete-dog?key=X&id=Y` | Admin — remove a community dog (its `communityDogs` entry + `img:`/`slug:` keys). For pulling non-dog uploads. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. Consumed by `admin.html`. |
| GET | `/admin-migrate-general?key=X&commit=1` | One-shot Phase 6 migration. Walks the user keyspace; for each `tier === 'general'` (legacy pre-2026-05-26 unlimited-bones purchaser): sets `tier='free'`, `bones = max(current, 2500)`, preserves `paidSku='general'`. **Dry-run by default**; pass `commit=1` to actually write. **The `tier === 'general'` bypass this enabled removing was removed 2026-06-23.** Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |
| GET | `/admin-grant-goodwill?key=X[&bones=1000][&days=N][&commit=1]` | **Added 2026-06-23 (free-to-enter pivot).** One-shot goodwill grant for legacy $3.99 `premium` buyers (`paidSku === 'premium'`), who paid for what is now free. Adds `bones` (default 1000) once per user (guarded by `goodwill:<userId>`) and emails `sendBenefitsChangeEmail`. `days` optionally limits to buyers whose `paidAt` is within N days (0 = all). **Dry-run by default**; `commit=1` writes + sends. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |
| GET | `/admin-migrate-monthly?key=X[&commit=1]` | **Added 2026-06-16 (`afb1427`).** One-shot weekly→monthly race transition. Seeds each community dog's `seasonBones` from its all-time `totalBones` so the new monthly board isn't empty, and stamps the current month's `seasonId` so `ensureSeason()` won't immediately roll over. **Dry-run by default**; pass `commit=1` to write. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. **Run-against-prod status not confirmed by docs.** |
| GET | `/admin-ai-test?key=X` | Throwaway diagnostic — probes the Cloudflare AI REST endpoint and runs resnet-50 on a known dog image. Used to verify the classifier (audit #38). Safe to delete now that the classifier fix shipped (`15e64ef`). Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |
| GET | `/admin-sentry-test?key=X` | Throwaway diagnostic — emits a test event through `reportToSentry()` to confirm server-side error reporting works end-to-end (audit High-3). Safe to delete after smoke-testing. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |
| GET | `/unsubscribe?x=<userId>.<token>` | One-click unsubscribe for marketing/recap emails (CAN-SPAM). **Single opaque param as of 2026-06-04 (UX-audit H1)** — the old two-param `?u=X&t=Y` form had its 2nd `=` corrupted by the mail pipeline; handler still accepts the legacy form. Token = first 16 hex chars of `SHA-256(userId + ':' + RESEND_API_KEY + ':unsub_v1')` — unforgeable, deterministic, no extra storage. Sets `user.unsubscribed=true` (idempotent), returns a small HTML confirmation page. |
| GET | `/version` | **Added 2026-06-04; LIVE in prod as of 2026-06-11** (committed in `9a6f2e3`, deployed with the leaderboard PartyKit deploy — prod returns `{"commit":"6745695","dirty":false,...}`, verified 2026-06-12). Returns the baked `BUILD_INFO` (`{commit, dirty, deployedAt}`) stamped at deploy time. Used by `npm run check-deploy` to detect prod↔repo drift (UX-audit H1/H2 root cause). |
| GET | `/admin-backfill-slugs?key=X[&commit=1]` | **Added 2026-06-04 (UX-audit L4); committed `9a6f2e3` + deployed 2026-06-11.** One-shot — assigns a slug + stores the `slug:` mapping for any aired community dog missing one (e.g. pre-slug-guarantee "Skeeterino"), so they get SSR `/d/{slug}` + per-dog OG. Dry-run by default; pass `commit=1` to write. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. **Not yet run with `commit=1`** — Skeeterino still has `slug: null` in `/leaderboard` as of 2026-06-12. |
| GET | `/testimonials` | **Added 2026-06-01.** Public — approved testimonials for the landing-page rail. `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`. Returns `{ok, testimonials: [{id, text, username, dogName, slug}]}`. |
| POST | `/inbound-email?secret=X` | **Added 2026-06-01.** Generic inbound-email webhook (Resend Inbound / Postmark / CloudMailin shape: `{from, subject, text, html?}`). Parses `[ref:TOK-xxxxxxxx]` token in subject/body to match a dog, strips quoted reply + signatures, sanitizes to ≤280 chars, stores as pending testimonial, alerts James. **Fail-closed**: 403 if `INBOUND_WEBHOOK_SECRET` unset. |
| GET | `/admin-testimonials?key=X` | **Added 2026-06-01.** Returns `{pending, approved, rejected}` arrays. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. Consumed by `admin.html`. |
| POST | `/admin-testimonial-action` | **Added 2026-06-01.** Body `{key, id, action: 'approve'\|'reject'\|'delete', edited?}`. `edited` overrides text on approve (original preserved as `original` field). Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |
| POST | `/admin-add-testimonial` | **Added 2026-06-01.** Manual paste — body `{key, dogId, text, status?}`. Defaults to `approved`. Used to seed the first 3-5 testimonials before inbound is wired, and as fallback for parsing misses. Requires `ADMIN_KEY`. |

> **Storage alarm (generalized 2026-05-26):** `server.js` arms a PartyKit storage alarm via `scheduleNextWakeup()`, which picks the soonest of (a) any pending RSVP reminder T-60min or T-5min, and (b) the daily-audit horizon (last audit + 24h). `onAlarm` calls `processSlotReminders()` first, then runs `computeAudit()` if 24h have elapsed, then re-arms. Audit Critical-6 + Phase 5 reminders. Old `scheduleAuditAlarm()` is kept as a back-compat shim. **Cadence split (2026-06-03, `9e0222b`):** the audit + per-user "you paid but haven't uploaded" nudge stay daily (still gated once-per-user by `nudged:<userId>`), but the **admin summary email** (`sendStuckUserAdminAlert`) now throttles to once per 7 days via `lastStuckSummaryAt` — and any user in `stuckResolutions` is filtered out upstream, so resolved rows never re-page James.

## WebSocket Message Types

**Server → client (handled in `app.js`):** `sync`, `newdog`, `chat`, `bone`, `viewers`, `intermission`, `communityCount`, `totalFans`, `boneBalance` (authoritative bones for the connection), `needTopUp` (rejected bone — out of balance), `voteResult` (reply to `voteOwnDog`: `{ok, dogId, ...}` or `{ok:false, reason}`)

**Client → server (handled in `party/server.js`):** `join` (now carries optional `token` to authenticate the connection — server resolves to a user and caches `{userId, bones, tier, username}` on the connection), `chat`, `bone` (server-enforced balance: legacy `tier === 'general'` bypassed until migration, everyone else decrements `bones`, refused with `needTopUp` if zero), `voteOwnDog` (added `d5d36d4`, 2026-06-22 — owner casts votes for their own dog directly, independent of who's on stage; spends bones via `spendBonesAsVotes()`)

> **Note:** `intermission` is disabled as of 2026-05-19 — the server-side trigger is commented out in `party/server.js` ("endless dog rotation"). The `startIntermission()` / `endIntermission()` machinery is retained but dormant on both server and client (`app.js`).

## Key JS Functions (index.html)

- `openEmailModal(tier)` — Email capture before checkout
- `enterShow(tier, email)` — Stripe checkout (paid) or direct register (free)
- `playCurtainAndGo(tier)` — Curtain animation → redirect to show.html
- `animateCounter(id, target)` — Animated number counters
- `startDogEntry()` — Open the upload modal; called by all "Enter Your Dog" CTAs; `pendingDogTier` (module-level) is `'free'` for normal entry (no checkout — registers free + auto-uploads on show entry) or `'premium_plus'` for the $5.99 Top Dog button (which goes through checkout). The `premium` $3.99 path is retired; the slot picker only shows when `pendingDogTier === 'premium_plus'`.
- `getChosenSlotAt()` — Returns the picker's chosen `slotAt` timestamp, or `null` for "show me now"
- `populateSlotDateSelect()` / `populateSlotTimeSelect()` — Build the date + time pickers from "now" forward

## Pricing Tiers (FREE-TO-ENTER model, 2026-06-23)

**Entry is now FREE for everyone. Money only buys bones.** The $3.99 `premium` "Enter Your Dog" SKU is RETIRED. See `free-to-enter-migration-2026-06-23.md` for the full change set + deploy/prod-action checklist. `BONES_ON_REGISTER` dropped 250→**50**; `BONES_TOPDOG = 1000` added.

1. **Dog Fan** — Free, no account. Watch only.
2. **Dog Entry** — Free (SKU `free`). Register → chat, **upload one dog** (cert page), **50 bones**. `/upload-dog` is no longer premium-gated; any registered user may upload one dog.
3. **Dog Entry Pro ($1.99, SKU `general`)** — Everything in Dog Entry **+ 250 bones**. (Same SKU as the repeatable $1.99 bones pack.)
4. **Top Dog ($5.99, SKU `premium_plus`)** — Everything **+ 1000 bones + slot booking + 3× on-stage duration**. Slot booking is gated to `paidSku === 'premium_plus'` (legacy `'premium'` $3.99 buyers keep slot rights). Sets `tier='premium'`.

Legacy notes: the `tier === 'general'` unlimited-bones bypass was **removed** (run `/admin-migrate-general?...&commit=1` on prod BEFORE deploying so grandfathered users get 2500 bones first). Legacy $3.99 `premium` buyers: goodwill bones via new `GET /admin-grant-goodwill?key=X[&bones=1000][&days=N][&commit=1]` (grants once per user + emails `sendBenefitsChangeEmail`). **Hardcoded-facts sync set still applies** — prices/bones live in `RESPONSIVE_BOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (server.js), `llms.txt`, and `brand-voice.md`; update together.

## Cloud automation migration (2026-07-03)

Recurring tasks moved off James's Mac into GitHub Actions (this repo). Workflows: `free-to-enter-tracker.yml` (08:00 UTC daily — `scripts/track_free_to_enter.py` appends `free-to-enter-tracking.csv`, commits, Telegrams the deltas), `wiki-update.yml` (Mon 04:00 UTC — CLAUDE.md maintenance per `prompts/wiki-update.md`), `breed-outreach.yml` (weekdays 07:00 UTC — Claude researches prospects per `prompts/breed-outreach.md`, then `scripts/gmail_create_drafts.py` creates Gmail DRAFTS via API; James still reviews + sends), `ads-search1-report.yml` (repository_dispatch receiver — a Google Ads Script (`scripts/ads-search1-google-ads-script.js`) pushes Search-1 weekly stats from Google's infra; Claude writes the insight report; Monday watchdog alerts if the script goes quiet). All reports go to Telegram (`scripts/notify_telegram.sh`, secrets TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN + TELEGRAM_OPS_CHAT_ID). The old local Cowork scheduled tasks (tracker, wiki, outreach stager, Ads browser-scrape report) are RETIRED. `.vercelignore` now also excludes `*.csv`, `prompts`, `.github`, `data`.

> **⚠️ MIGRATION STILL NOT DEPLOYED (re-verified 2026-07-13).** The `.github/workflows/`, `prompts/`, and new `scripts/*.py`/`.sh`/`.js` files remain **untracked in the local repo and absent from origin** (first verified 2026-07-06; unchanged a week later even though the pale-theme commits were pushed 2026-07-08 and `.gitignore`/`.vercelignore` edits sit modified-but-uncommitted). GitHub Actions cannot run workflows that were never pushed, so **none of the four cloud automations are live**. Evidence: `free-to-enter-tracking.csv` still has zero GH-committed rows; daily rows through 2026-07-11 all came from the *local* tracker task (still firing despite being marked retired — currently the only thing preventing a data gap). **To complete the migration: commit + push `.github/`, `prompts/`, the new `scripts/` files, and the modified `.gitignore`/`.vercelignore`; add the GitHub repo secrets (TELEGRAM_*, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, Gmail creds); verify a workflow run; only then retire the local tasks.** Note the local `dogshow-wiki-update` scheduled task also still runs (this warning was last refreshed by its 2026-07-13 run) — once `wiki-update.yml` is live the two will duplicate Monday updates.
